# feat(runtime): implement ProgressUpdate contract for long-running nodes

<!-- Generated by SessionEnd hook from a commit matching a journal-worthy pattern.
     Next session: review, then promote to a real journal entry OR delete. -->

**Commit**: `8f137a3c1be2` — feat(runtime): implement ProgressUpdate contract for long-running nodes

**Body**:

Issue #374 — long-running nodes (AI inference, bulk DB ops) provide no
intermediate status. Operators and UIs have no visibility into progress.

This adds a ProgressUpdate dataclass, ProgressRegistry for callback
management, and a report_progress() convenience function that nodes
can call from run() without requiring code changes to existing nodes.

The context variable plumbing in Node.execute() and execute_async()
sets up _current_node_id so report_progress() discovers the active
node automatically. When no registry is active, report_progress() is
a silent no-op — full backward compatibility.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
